title: 8-Week Learn → Interview Roadmap
description: Depth-first learning first, then interview practice. Each week links to deep topics.

items.title: Week 1 — Foundations
items.body:
Learn: Programming Fundamentals (SOLID), Immutability, Operating Systems basics.
Practice: explain SRP with a real service split; write an immutable Money type.
Quiz each topic to ≥80%.

items.title: Week 2 — Concurrency & Patterns
items.body:
Learn: Concurrency, Design Patterns (Factory/Decorator/Singleton/Observer).
Practice: fix a lost-update counter; decorate a repository with metrics.

items.title: Week 3 — Data & APIs
items.body:
Learn: Databases, APIs, Idempotency, Caching.
Practice: N+1 fix; Idempotency-Key design; cache-aside with stampede control.

items.title: Week 4 — Distributed building blocks
items.body:
Learn: Messaging & Streaming, Networking, System Design.
Practice: outbox + idempotent consumer; sketch URL shortener with cache + DB.

items.title: Week 5 — Security & Auth
items.body:
Learn: Security, SSL/JWT/OAuth, Cloud & DevOps basics.
Practice: JWT validation checklist; pick OAuth flow for SPA vs cron.

items.title: Week 6 — Quality & Ops
items.body:
Learn: Testing, TDD, Observability, Debugging.
Practice: red→green→refactor on a domain rule; define RED dashboard + SLO.

items.title: Week 7 — DSA patterns + design prompts
items.body:
Drill: hash maps, two pointers, sliding window, BFS/DFS, light DP.
Design: rate limiter, chat, notifications. Always mention failure modes & metrics.

items.title: Week 8 — Mocks & behavioral
items.body:
Full loops: coding + design + STAR stories (ownership, conflict, failure).
Review weak deep topics; retake quizzes; polish scoreboard gaps.

title: Most-Asked DSA Patterns
description: Pattern recognition beats memorizing 300 problems. Learn Algorithms deeply, then drill these.

items.title: Hash map lookups
items.body:
Classic: Two Sum, anagram groups, subarray sum equals K.
Approach: value→index or frequency map; O(n) average.
Say time/space out loud.

items.title: Two pointers
items.body:
Classic: sorted two-sum, container with most water, valid palindrome.
Approach: left/right on sorted array or string; move the side that can improve the answer.

items.title: Sliding window
items.body:
Classic: longest substring without repeat, min window substring.
Approach: expand right, shrink left while invariant broken; O(n).

items.title: BFS / DFS
items.body:
Classic: level order, number of islands, clone graph.
BFS for shortest path in unweighted graphs; DFS for components/backtracking.

items.title: Binary search
items.body:
Classic: search insert position, first bad version, rotated array.
Invariant: answer in [lo, hi]; be careful with mid and boundaries.

items.title: 1D / 2D DP (medium)
items.body:
Classic: climb stairs, house robber, unique paths, coin change.
Define state, transition, base cases; start recursive+memo then tabulate.

title: System Design Prompts
description: Framework: clarify → capacity → high-level → deep dive → trade-offs/ops. Link building-block topics.

items.title: Design a URL shortener
items.body:
Clarify: read/write ratio, custom aliases, expiry.
Core: base62 ids, DB mapping, Redis cache for redirects, analytics async.
Ops: cache stampede on hot links, rate limit creates.

items.title: Design a rate limiter
items.body:
Token bucket vs sliding window; Redis counters; per-user/IP/API-key.
Fail open vs closed; where to enforce (gateway vs service).

items.title: Design a chat app
items.body:
WebSockets/gateway; message store; fan-out; online presence; push.
Ordering per conversation; at-least-once delivery + idempotent clients.

items.title: Design a notification system
items.body:
Ingest events → queue → channel workers (email/SMS/push); templates; preferences; retries/DLQ.
Idempotency so users are not spammed on replay.

items.title: Design a RAG support chatbot
items.body:
Ingest/chunk/embed → vector DB → retrieve/rerank → LLM with citations.
Cost/latency budgets, evals, refusal when retrieval weak.

title: Behavioral STAR Bank
description: Prepare stories with Situation, Task, Action, Result (metrics). Behavioral rounds are decisive at many companies.

items.title: Ownership / delivered under ambiguity
items.body:
S/T: unclear requirements, deadline risk.
A: clarified scope, sliced MVP, communicated trade-offs, owned rollout.
R: shipped on time with measurable impact (latency, revenue, error rate).

items.title: Technical disagreement
items.body:
S/T: disagreed on SQL vs NoSQL / sync vs async.
A: wrote spike, compared data with constraints, facilitated decision doc.
R: chose option X; prevented incident Y; team alignment.

items.title: Production failure / incident
items.body:
S/T: outage or bug you contributed to.
A: mitigated, RCA, fix, prevention (tests, alerts, runbook).
R: MTTR, recurrence prevented — honesty + learning matter.

items.title: Tight deadline
items.body:
S/T: immovable date.
A: cut scope consciously, parallelized, protected quality on critical path.
R: delivered core value; followed up on debt.

items.title: Learning agility
items.body:
S/T: new domain/tech under pressure.
A: structured learning plan, mentor/docs, small production slice.
R: independent ownership within N weeks; concrete outcome.

title: Quick-fire CS Fundamentals
description: Short prompts common in campus/service rounds. Learn the linked topics for depth.

items.title: What does Atomicity guarantee?
items.body:
All-or-nothing transaction outcomes — partial updates roll back.
Learn deeply in Databases.

items.title: TCP vs UDP one-liner?
items.body:
TCP: reliable ordered streams. UDP: best-effort datagrams.
Learn deeply in Networking.

items.title: Process vs thread?
items.body:
Processes isolate address spaces; threads share heap/memory inside a process.
Learn deeply in OS + Concurrency.

items.title: CAP theorem in one sentence?
items.body:
During a partition, you trade Consistency vs Availability.
Learn deeply in System Design.

items.title: Why prefer RS256/JWKS over shared HS256?
items.body:
Only the issuer holds the private key; services verify with public JWKS — no shared secret sprawl.
Learn deeply in Auth & TLS.
